title: T'choupi à l'école — la journée du pyjama
why: A children's cartoon: clear, slow speech and the pictures carry the meaning. The gentlest possible listening — perfect for your very first weeks.
mission: Watch once with no subtitles — just follow the pictures.
Watch again and catch the words you already know.
Say out loud, in French, what you think is happening.

title: French greetings every beginner needs (Super Easy French)
why: Real French, spoken slowly, with French + English subtitles. The greetings you'll use on day one in Paris.

questions.q: « Salut ! » — when do you use it?
options: With friends and people your age
In a formal email to a stranger
questions.explain: « Salut » is informal — great with friends, but not with your boss on day one.

questions.q: Which greeting do you use in the evening?
questions.explain: « Bonsoir » = good evening. « Bonne nuit » is only when you leave to go to sleep.

questions.q: « Enchanté(e) » means « nice to meet you ».
questions.explain: Yes — you say it the first time you meet someone.

options: A way to say goodbye only
questions.explain: « Coucou » is warm and casual — exactly why this site is called Coucou. 😊

title: TV5MONDE — A1 listening exercises (auto-marked)
why: Real TV clips cut to beginner length, each with built-in comprehension questions that mark themselves. 2000+ free exercises.
mission: Pick any video, watch it twice before answering.
Do the exercise — it corrects you instantly.
Note 3 new words in your tracker.

title: Easy French — real street interviews (FR + EN subtitles)
why: Real people answering simple questions on the streets of Paris. Start with the « Super Easy French » ones, then the normal-speed ones.
mission: Choose a « Super Easy French » video first.
Watch with both subtitles, then again with French only.
Shadow one sentence — copy the rhythm out loud.

title: TV5MONDE — A2 listening exercises (auto-marked)
why: The next step up: slightly faster clips, still with self-correcting comprehension questions.

title: T'choupi — 1-hour cartoon compilation (passive listening)
why: Put it on while you cook or tidy up. Passive exposure trains your ear to French rhythm — no pressure to understand every word.
mission: Don't translate — let the sound wash over you.
Catch one new word per episode and write it down.

title: InnerFrench — slow, clear French on real topics (B1 → B2)
why: Made for learners: natural but clear French about culture, history and society. The single best bridge across the intermediate plateau. Full transcripts at innerfrench.com.
mission: Listen once without the transcript.
Listen again following the transcript on innerfrench.com.
Tell your AI tutor what the episode was about, in French.

title: TV5MONDE — B1 listening exercises (auto-marked)
why: Authentic news and culture clips with comprehension questions — very close to the DELF listening format.

title: RFI — Le journal en français facile (news + transcript)
why: A 10-minute world-news bulletin every day in simplified French, with the transcript and self-correcting exercises. Builds the news vocabulary DELF B2 loves.
mission: Listen first with the transcript hidden.
Re-read with the transcript and look up 5 words.
Summarise one news item to your tutor in French.

title: TV5MONDE — B2 listening exercises (auto-marked)
why: Full-speed authentic broadcasts with DELF-style questions. The closest free thing to the real B2 listening paper.
mission: Watch once at normal speed — no pausing, like the exam.
Answer, then re-watch to see what you missed.
Replay the hard passages and shadow them out loud.

title: RFI Français Facile — daily news on YouTube
why: The same news at full authentic pace. Train your ear for the fast, smeared French of real radio.

title: Arte — documentaries & reportages (B2 / C1 stretch)
why: Real French TV made for natives: documentaries on science, society and culture. Hard, but the fastest way to push past B2 toward C1.
mission: Pick a short reportage on a topic you already like.
Use French subtitles (sous-titres) as a crutch at first.
Drop the subtitles once you can follow the gist.
